title: Model Evaluation & Optimization
grading_philosophy: Module 4 emphasizes technical rigor and systematic optimization. Students should demonstrate: proper metric selection, cross-validation mastery, overfitting diagnosis, hyperparameter tuning, and comprehensive model evaluation. Quality of process matters as much as final results.

sessions.title: Classification Metrics Deep Dive
sessions.purpose: Master precision, recall, F1, confusion matrices, and ROC curves
sessions.assessment_type: Re-evaluation of Module 2 models with comprehensive metrics

criteria.description: Calculates all classification metrics correctly for spam detector

excellent.description: Correctly calculates accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score for spam detector. Generates confusion matrix. Creates ROC curve with AUC score. All metrics interpreted correctly in business context.

good.description: Calculates 4-5 metrics correctly. Creates confusion matrix or ROC curve. Basic interpretation provided.

adequate.description: Calculates 2-3 metrics. Confusion matrix present. Minimal interpretation.

poor.description: Fewer than 2 metrics calculated or incorrect calculations.

sklearn_imports: from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, confusion_matrix, roc_auc_score, roc_curve

criteria.description: Selects appropriate metric for business context and justifies choice

excellent.description: Clearly identifies which metric matters most for spam detection (precision to avoid false positives). Justifies threshold selection. Discusses tradeoffs. Provides business recommendations.

good.description: Identifies appropriate metric. Provides justification. Discusses one tradeoff.

adequate.description: Identifies metric with basic reasoning. Minimal tradeoff discussion.

poor.description: Wrong metric selected or no business justification.

criteria.description: Creates clear, professional visualizations of metrics

excellent.description: Creates: (1) Confusion matrix heatmap with annotations, (2) ROC curve with AUC, (3) Precision-Recall curve, (4) Metrics comparison bar chart. All well-formatted with titles and labels.

good.description: Creates 3 of 4 required visualizations with proper formatting.

poor.description: Fewer than 2 visualizations or poor formatting.

evidence_required: ROC curve with diagonal reference line

sessions.grading_notes: This session reuses spam detector from Module 2. Focus on whether student can apply advanced metrics beyond basic accuracy. Look for understanding of when precision vs recall matters.

sessions.title: Regression Metrics Deep Dive
sessions.purpose: Master MAE, MSE, RMSE, R², and residual analysis
sessions.assessment_type: Re-evaluation of Module 3 stock predictor with comprehensive metrics

criteria.description: Calculates all regression metrics correctly for stock predictor

excellent.description: Correctly calculates MAE, MSE, RMSE, R², and MAPE. Interprets each metric correctly. Explains differences between metrics.

good.description: Calculates 4 of 5 metrics correctly with basic interpretation.

poor.description: Fewer than 2 metrics or incorrect calculations.

evidence_required: R² score with correct interpretation (not 'percentage correct')

criteria.description: Performs comprehensive residual analysis to diagnose model issues

excellent.description: Creates: (1) Actual vs Predicted scatter, (2) Residuals plot, (3) Residual distribution histogram, (4) Q-Q plot. Identifies patterns (heteroscedasticity, non-linearity, outliers). Documents findings.

good.description: Creates 3 of 4 residual plots. Identifies one pattern. Basic documentation.

adequate.description: Creates 1-2 residual plots. Minimal pattern analysis.

poor.description: No residual analysis or one incomplete plot.

criteria.description: Analyzes which stocks predict well vs poorly and explains why

excellent.description: Calculates metrics for each stock separately. Identifies best and worst performers. Provides hypotheses for performance differences (volatility, market sector, data quality). Creates comparison visualization.

good.description: Analyzes 3+ stocks. Identifies best/worst. Basic reasoning provided.

poor.description: No per-stock analysis or single stock only.

sessions.grading_notes: Residual analysis is new for students. Focus on whether they can create the plots and identify basic patterns. Don't expect statistical expertise, but reward thoughtful observations.

sessions.title: Cross-Validation
sessions.purpose: Implement K-Fold, Stratified, and Time Series CV correctly
sessions.assessment_type: Apply CV to previous models and interpret results

criteria.description: Correctly implements 5-fold CV on spam detector

excellent.description: Implements 5-fold CV using sklearn. Reports mean ± std for all metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1). Creates box plot showing score distribution across folds. Interprets variance.

good.description: Implements CV correctly. Reports mean ± std for 2-3 metrics. Basic visualization.

adequate.description: Implements CV. Reports mean scores only.

evidence_required: Mean and standard deviation for each metric

criteria.description: Applies stratified CV to churn predictor and compares to regular CV

excellent.description: Implements both regular and stratified K-Fold. Shows class distribution in each fold for both methods. Demonstrates stratified maintains class balance. Compares results. Explains why stratification matters for imbalanced data.

good.description: Implements both methods. Shows class distribution. Basic comparison.

adequate.description: Implements stratified CV only. Shows it maintains balance.

criteria.description: Implements forward-chaining CV for stock predictor without data leakage

excellent.description: Implements TimeSeriesSplit correctly. Shows training set grows in each split. Verifies no future data used for training (no leakage). Compares to random split showing problem. Creates visualization of splits.

good.description: Implements TimeSeriesSplit correctly. Shows growing training sets. Compares to random split.

adequate.description: Implements TimeSeriesSplit. Basic verification of correctness.

poor.description: Uses random CV on time series (data leakage!) or incorrect implementation.

evidence_required: Visualization of split structure (training/test sizes per fold)
Explanation of why time-aware CV matters

technical_requirements.no_shuffle: Must NOT shuffle data before splitting

sessions.grading_notes: Time series CV is crucial and commonly done wrong. Award full points only if student demonstrates understanding of no-future-data principle. Partial credit if implementation works but lacks understanding of why.

sessions.title: Diagnosing Overfitting & Underfitting
sessions.purpose: Identify and fix overfitting using learning curves and regularization

criteria.description: Generates and interprets learning curves correctly

excellent.description: Creates learning curves showing training and validation scores vs training size. Correctly identifies overfitting (large gap) or underfitting (converged at low value). Plots are well-formatted with legend. Provides interpretation with recommendations.

good.description: Creates learning curves. Identifies overfitting or underfitting. Basic interpretation.

adequate.description: Creates learning curves but misinterprets or provides minimal analysis.

poor.description: No learning curves or completely misinterprets.

evidence_required: Learning curve plot (train and val scores)
Gap analysis between train and val curves

technical_requirements.plot_elements: Both training and validation curves on same plot

criteria.description: Creates validation curve to find optimal model complexity

excellent.description: Creates validation curve varying complexity parameter (e.g., max_depth for tree). Identifies optimal complexity where validation score peaks. Shows overfitting after this point. Clear visualization with train/val curves.

good.description: Creates validation curve. Identifies approximate optimal point. Basic visualization.

adequate.description: Creates validation curve but unclear optimal point or minimal interpretation.

poor.description: No validation curve or completely misinterprets.

evidence_required: Explanation of overfitting at high complexity

criteria.description: Applies L1, L2, or Elastic Net regularization to reduce overfitting

excellent.description: Tests all three regularization types (L1/Lasso, L2/Ridge, Elastic Net). Compares performance of each. Shows reduced overfitting (smaller train/test gap). Selects best approach. Creates before/after comparison showing improvement.

good.description: Tests 2 regularization types. Shows reduced overfitting. Basic comparison.

adequate.description: Tests 1 regularization type. Shows some improvement.

poor.description: No regularization applied or incorrect implementation.

technical_requirements.alpha_tuning: Should experiment with different regularization strengths

sessions.grading_notes: This session is about diagnosis AND fixing. Don't give full credit if student only identifies overfitting but doesn't successfully reduce it. Look for train/test gap reduction after regularization.

sessions.title: Hyperparameter Tuning
sessions.purpose: Systematically optimize hyperparameters using Grid Search and Random Search
sessions.assessment_type: Optimize stock predictor Random Forest model

criteria.description: Implements Grid Search with 5-fold CV to find optimal hyperparameters

excellent.description: Implements GridSearchCV with: (1) 3+ hyperparameters (n_estimators, max_depth, min_samples_split), (2) 3+ values per parameter, (3) 5-fold CV, (4) Appropriate scoring metric. Reports best parameters, best score, and improvement over default. Creates hyperparameter importance visualization.

good.description: Implements GridSearchCV with 2-3 hyperparameters. Uses CV. Reports best parameters and score. Basic improvement shown.

adequate.description: Implements GridSearchCV with 1-2 hyperparameters. Basic results reported.

poor.description: No Grid Search or incorrect implementation (e.g., no CV).

criteria.description: Implements Random Search and compares efficiency to Grid Search

excellent.description: Implements RandomizedSearchCV with same parameter space. Sets n_iter=30. Compares: (1) Best scores found, (2) Computation time, (3) Parameters explored. Creates optimization history plot. Discusses when Random Search is better.

good.description: Implements RandomizedSearchCV. Compares best scores and time. Basic discussion.

adequate.description: Implements Random Search. Reports best score only.

poor.description: No Random Search or no comparison to Grid Search.

evidence_required: Discussion of Grid vs Random Search tradeoffs

technical_requirements.same_param_space: Use same parameters as Grid Search for fair comparison

criteria.description: Documents clear improvement from hyperparameter tuning

excellent.description: Creates comparison table showing: (1) Default model performance, (2) Grid Search optimized, (3) Random Search optimized. Shows 10%+ improvement in primary metric (R² or RMSE). Creates before/after visualization. Explains which hyperparameters had biggest impact.

good.description: Shows clear improvement (5-10%). Comparison table present. Basic visualization.

poor.description: No improvement shown or performance decreased (suggests error in implementation).

performance_targets.minimum_improvement: 5% improvement in R² or 5% reduction in RMSE
performance_targets.concerning_result: Performance decrease suggests overfitting to validation set

sessions.grading_notes: Hyperparameter tuning should lead to measurable improvement. If student shows no improvement or decrease, discuss potential issues: (1) Default parameters already optimal, (2) Search space too narrow, (3) Overfitting to validation set. Award partial credit if implementation correct even if improvement minimal.

sessions.title: Feature Selection & Model Comparison
sessions.purpose: Select optimal features and systematically compare models
sessions.assessment_type: Feature selection lab + Fraud Detection Capstone introduction

criteria.description: Analyzes feature importance and removes low-importance features

excellent.description: Calculates feature importance for Random Forest model. Visualizes top 20 features. Identifies threshold for low-importance features. Creates reduced feature set. Compares model performance: full features vs selected features. Documents speed and accuracy tradeoff.

good.description: Calculates and visualizes feature importance. Removes low-importance features. Basic comparison.

adequate.description: Calculates feature importance. Basic visualization. Minimal comparison.

poor.description: No feature importance analysis or incorrect implementation.

criteria.description: Applies RFE to find optimal feature subset

excellent.description: Implements RFE using sklearn. Tests different numbers of features (e.g., 5, 10, 15, 20). Creates plot showing performance vs number of features. Identifies optimal subset. Compares RFE results to importance-based selection. Lists selected features with interpretation.

good.description: Implements RFE. Tests 2-3 feature counts. Identifies optimal subset. Basic comparison.

adequate.description: Implements RFE with one feature count. Basic results.

evidence_required: Performance vs number of features plot

technical_requirements.estimator: Use model with feature_importances_ or coef_ attribute
technical_requirements.cv: Use RFECV for automatic optimal feature count selection

criteria.description: Removes highly correlated features and analyzes impact

excellent.description: Creates correlation matrix heatmap. Identifies feature pairs with correlation > 0.9. Removes redundant features from each pair. Compares model performance before/after removal. Shows reduction in model complexity without sacrificing accuracy. Documents which features were removed and why.

good.description: Creates correlation matrix. Identifies high correlations. Removes features. Basic comparison.

adequate.description: Creates correlation matrix. Identifies some correlations. Minimal removal.

poor.description: No correlation analysis or incorrect implementation.

evidence_required: List of removed features with justification

sessions.grading_notes: Feature selection is an important optimization step. Focus on systematic approach rather than achieving specific performance targets. Award full credit if student demonstrates all three methods correctly even if improvement is minimal.

required_completions: All Module 4 techniques applied to previous models
At least 10% model improvement shown through optimization
bonus_opportunities: Exceeds 15% improvement in optimized model (+2 points)
Creates interactive dashboard for model comparison (+2 points)

instructor_guidelines.grading_approach: Module 4 is technical and rigorous. Look for: (1) Correct implementation of techniques, (2) Proper use of cross-validation, (3) Demonstrated improvement, (4) Clear documentation of process. Don't penalize if improvement is modest - ML is inherently uncertain.

common_issues.session_1: Students may confuse precision with accuracy. Use confusion matrix to clarify. Remind that for imbalanced data, accuracy is misleading.
common_issues.session_2: R² interpretation is often wrong ('not percentage correct'). Emphasize variance explained concept.
common_issues.session_3: Time series CV is frequently done incorrectly. CRITICAL: No shuffling, training on past only. Award partial credit if conceptually correct even if sklearn usage has minor bugs.
common_issues.session_4: Learning curves may show different patterns than expected. Focus on whether student can interpret their results, not match textbook patterns.
common_issues.session_5: Grid Search can be computationally expensive. If student uses smaller parameter grid due to compute constraints, that's acceptable with documentation.
common_issues.session_6: Feature selection may not always improve performance. Award full credit if methods applied correctly even if 'no improvement' is the valid conclusion.

time_expectations.struggling_students: 40-50 hours (encourage focusing on key techniques, can skip optional advanced topics)

revision_needed_indicators: No improvement or performance decrease without explanation
Hyperparameter tuning without CV (overfitting to validation)
Code doesn't run or has major errors

must_understand: Precision vs Recall tradeoff and when each matters
Time series CV must not use future data
Hyperparameter tuning requires CV to prevent overfitting
common_misconceptions: 'Accuracy is always the best metric' - FALSE for imbalanced data
'More features = better performance' - FALSE, can cause overfitting
'Grid Search always finds best hyperparameters' - FALSE, limited to search space
'Regularization always improves performance' - FALSE, can cause underfitting

feedback_guidelines.praise: Highlight systematic approach, correct technique application, thoughtful interpretation
feedback_guidelines.improve: Guide toward proper CV usage, better metric selection, clearer documentation
feedback_guidelines.encourage: Module 4 is challenging but essential for production ML. Mastery here separates hobbyists from professionals.

alignment_with_quizzes.session_1_quiz: 9 questions on precision, recall, F1, confusion matrix, ROC/AUC (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_2_quiz: 9 questions on MAE, MSE, RMSE, R², residual analysis (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_3_quiz: 9 questions on K-Fold, stratified, time series CV (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_4_quiz: 9 questions on overfitting, learning curves, regularization (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_5_quiz: 9 questions on hyperparameters, Grid/Random Search, Bayesian optimization (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_6_quiz: 9 questions on feature importance, RFE, model selection (70% passing)

success_criteria: Proper handling of 0.172% fraud rate (severe imbalance)

skills_carried_forward: Cross-validation mastery (used in unsupervised learning evaluation)
Feature selection techniques (useful for dimensionality reduction)
Systematic optimization approach (applies to clustering parameter tuning)
preparation_for_module_5.mindset_shift: Module 4: 'Optimize supervised models' → Module 5: 'Discover patterns without labels'
